question_text: Which of the following best explains why we always see the same face of the Moon from Earth?

options.label: A
options.text: The Moon does not rotate at all

options.label: B
options.text: The Moon's rotation period equals its orbital period around Earth due to tidal forces slowing its rotation over time

options.label: C
options.text: Earth's gravity is so strong it prevents the Moon from rotating

options.label: D
options.text: The Moon orbits Earth so quickly that we cannot see it rotate

explanation: This is called tidal locking. Earth's gravity pulls on the Moon more on the side facing Earth, which slowed the Moon's rotation until it matched its orbital period—now both take 27.3 days, so the same face always points at us.
